DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL ARE SANCTIONED FOR MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. (Amended) Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS W. CHAFFEE, ESQ. of Morris Polich & Purdy LLP, Tel No. 415-984-8500 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF MARY HABER'S (Amended) Motion To Compel Defendants Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions, Plaintiff's unopposed motion to compel is granted. Defendant shall serve verified, further responses without objections, no later than December 1, 2015, to Plaintiff's First Set of Form Interrogatories. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is granted. Defendant and Defendant's counsel are sanctioned for misuse of the discovery process. Defendant and its counsel are jointly and severally sanctioned in the amount of $1,225, payable to Plaintiff no later than December 21, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Conor Moore, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
